activities_projects.title: Measuring Shadow Length During the Day
activities_projects.objective: Observe Sun’s apparent motion using shadow length
steps: Place the stick vertically on the ground
Measure shadow length in morning, noon, and evening
Record and compare shadow lengths and directions
Graph shadow length vs time of day
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects Sun’s position with shadow length and direction quantitatively
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate measurement, recording, and graphing (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Observing Moon Rise and Set Times
activities_projects.objective: Understand Moon’s movement in the sky
steps: Record Moon rise and set times for several consecutive days
Note the change in timing each day
Analyze pattern in Moon’s apparent movement
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns how Moon rise/set changes daily due to orbital motion
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate observations and pattern analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Modeling Phases of the Moon with Geometry
activities_projects.objective: Understand Moon phases and illumination angles
steps: Place globe in center, hold Moon at different positions
Shine torch (Sun) on Moon
Observe and draw illuminated portion for each phase
Label waxing, waning, full, and new Moon
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects Moon phase to Earth-Moon-Sun geometry
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct illustration of Moon phases (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Simulating Solar Eclipse Angles
steps: Place Moon between torch and Earth at different distances
Observe shadow on Earth to simulate partial and total eclipses
Measure angles of alignment using protractor
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands solar eclipse geometry and shadow coverage
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate simulation and angle measurement (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Simulating Lunar Eclipse Angles
steps: Place Moon opposite Sun with Earth in center
Observe shadow cast on Moon
Measure angle of Sun-Earth-Moon alignment
Explain why lunar eclipse happens at full Moon
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects alignment and Moon phase to lunar eclipse
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct demonstration, measurements, and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Studying Seasonal Changes Using Globe Tilt
activities_projects.objective: Understand effect of Earth’s tilt on seasons
steps: Tilt globe at 23.5° to simulate Earth’s axis
Shine torch to simulate Sun at different positions
Observe illumination on different latitudes during revolution
Record observations for summer and winter hemispheres

activities_projects.title: Calculating Earth Rotation Speed
activities_projects.objective: Estimate Earth’s rotational speed at equator
steps: Measure circumference of globe
Use proportion to estimate Earth’s equatorial circumference (~40,000 km)
Divide by 24 hours to calculate speed (km/h)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects rotation to linear speed of Earth

activities_projects.title: Tracking Apparent Sun Path
steps: Mark Sun’s position at regular intervals (morning, noon, evening)
Draw Sun’s apparent path across the sky
Explain how path changes with seasons
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands Sun’s apparent motion and seasonal variation
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate path diagram and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Moon Orbit and Synodic Month Calculation
steps: Record Moon phases over one month
Calculate number of days between two similar phases
Compare with actual synodic month (~29.5 days)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects Moon phases to orbital period

activities_projects.title: Quiz on Earth, Moon, and Sun – Medium Level
steps: Prepare five questions involving shadow, rotation, and Moon phases
Prepare five questions on eclipses, seasons, and Earth tilt
Exchange with a friend and solve
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality and correctness of answers (10 marks)
